title: ComenGoRemover
description: A plugin that removes the join / leave messages with an extra addon
body:
ComenGoRemover: A Clean Server Communication Solution


ComenGoRemover is a lightweight yet powerful Minecraft server plugin designed to give administrators complete control over join and leave messages. Whether you want to eliminate distracting entry notifications entirely or customize them to match your server's aesthetic, ComenGoRemover offers a simple, configurable solution.

Key Features

Flexible Configuration - Choose to hide join messages, leave messages, or both

Custom Message Support - Create personalized join/leave messages with full color code support

Lightweight Design - Minimal impact on server performance

Simple Commands - Easy-to-use reload command for configuration changes

Permission-Based - Secure control over who can modify plugin settings


Why Choose ComenGoRemover?

Clean Chat Experience

In busy servers, constant join/leave notifications can overwhelm the chat, making important messages hard to follow. ComenGoRemover eliminates this clutter, creating a cleaner, more focused chat experience for your players.

Enhanced Privacy

Some players prefer not having their comings and goings announced to the entire server. This plugin respects player privacy while still allowing administrators to track activity through server logs.

Professional Appearance

For professional or roleplay servers, generic join/leave messages can break immersion. ComenGoRemover helps maintain your server's atmosphere by either removing these messages or replacing them with themed custom announcements.

Resource Efficiency

Unlike more complex chat management plugins that consume significant server resources, ComenGoRemover focuses on doing one thing extremely well, with minimal performance impact.

Easy Integration

ComenGoRemover works alongside other plugins without conflicts, making it an ideal addition to any server setup without requiring major configuration changes.
Installation


Download the ComenGoRemover.jar file

Place it in your server's plugins folder

Restart your server or use the plugin load command

The default configuration will be generated automatically

Modify the config.yml file to your preferences

Use /cgr reload to apply changes without restarting
